prompt:
Provide a band-pass filter design for a high-impedance signal source driving a 2000 ohm load. The insertion loss must be below 0.5 dB from 300 Hz to 1 kHz. A minimum of 3.5 dB attenuation is required at 200 Hz. A minimum of 9.5 dB attenuation is required at 3 kHz. Only one op-amp is permitted alongside resistors and capacitors; keep the BOM cost low.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Specify a passive RC band-pass network for a signal conditioning application. The passband extends from 3 kHz to 6 kHz with <= 1.0 dB insertion loss. The filter must attenuate 800 Hz by >= 11.5 dB and 20 kHz by >= 10.5 dB. Use only resistors and capacitors; keep the design as simple as possible.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a band-pass filter to select a frequency band before sampling. The passband spans 200 Hz to 800 Hz with <= 0.5 dB insertion loss. A minimum of 20.0 dB rejection is required at 20 Hz. A minimum of 11.5 dB rejection is required at 3 kHz. Use only resistors and capacitors; minimise part count.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a band-pass filter for a sensor with high output impedance. Signals between 2 kHz and 20 kHz must be passed with <= 1.0 dB loss. Signals at 400 Hz must be attenuated by at least 14.0 dB. Signals at 60 kHz must be attenuated by at least 9.5 dB. Use resistors, capacitors, and at most one op-amp; optimise for minimum part count.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Create a high-selectivity passive band-pass filter for a measurement system. Signals from 2 kHz to 20 kHz must pass with less than 1.0 dB loss. The circuit must provide at least 60.2 dB of rejection at 500 Hz and at least 20.0 dB of rejection at 200 kHz. No active components; keep the design as cost-effective as possible.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Design a band-pass filter with aggressive out-of-band rejection for a high-impedance sensor interface. The passband must span 700 Hz to 3 kHz with under 3.0 dB loss. Provide >= 9.7 dB of rejection at 400 Hz and >= 80.0 dB of rejection at 30 kHz. An op-amp is permitted to handle source impedance; use only passive components otherwise.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Provide a band-pass filter design with high out-of-band rejection using only passive components. The passband spans 900 Hz to 2 kHz with a maximum of 1.0 dB insertion loss. At 400 Hz, a minimum of 14.1 dB attenuation is required. At 6 kHz, a minimum of 47.7 dB attenuation is required. No active components; use the fewest resistors and capacitors that satisfy all constraints.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.

Specify a band-pass filter for a high-impedance source whose output feeds a 500 ohm stage. The passband must span 600 Hz to 2 kHz with less than 0.5 dB insertion loss. At 200 Hz, the filter must deliver at least 28.6 dB of attenuation. At 8 kHz, the filter must deliver at least 48.2 dB of attenuation. Restrict to R, C, and at most one op-amp; favour the lowest-cost design.

Provide the design as a SPICE netlist. The following node names are mandatory for automated simulation:
  - Input node   : VIN
  - Output node  : VOUT
  - Ground / 0 V : GND
Name all resistors with the prefix R (e.g. R1, R2), all capacitors with the prefix C (e.g. C1, C2), and any op-amp with the designator U1. Include a voltage source V1 connected between VIN and GND. End the netlist with a .END statement. Do not include a simulation command (.AC, .TRAN, etc.) in the netlist; the simulation will be configured externally.
